title: Western Blot — gel, transfer, blocking, antibody incubation and detection
title_en: Western Blot — gel, transfer, blocking, antibody incubation and detection
summary: SDS-PAGE gel run, protein transfer to nitrocellulose membrane, blocking, primary and secondary antibody incubation, and chemiluminescent detection.
summary_en: SDS-PAGE gel run, protein transfer to nitrocellulose membrane, blocking, primary and secondary antibody incubation, and chemiluminescent detection.

materials.name: Blocking buffer: 1X TBST with 5% w/v nonfat dry milk
Primary antibody dilution buffer: 1X TBST with 5% BSA

steps.text: Load the denatured protein samples and a molecular weight ladder into the wells of the SDS-PAGE gel.
steps.text_en: Load the denatured protein samples and a molecular weight ladder into the wells of the SDS-PAGE gel.
steps.verbatim: 1. Load the denatured protein samples and a molecular weight ladder into the wells of the SDS-PAGE gel.

steps.text: Run the gel at 120 V until the dye front reaches the bottom (approximately 90 min).
steps.text_en: Run the gel at 120 V until the dye front reaches the bottom (approximately 90 min).
steps.verbatim: 2. Run the gel at 120 V until the dye front reaches the bottom (approximately 90 min).

steps.text: Transfer the proteins from the gel onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V for 60 min in cold transfer buffer.
steps.text_en: Transfer the proteins from the gel onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V for 60 min in cold transfer buffer.
steps.verbatim: 3. Transfer the proteins from the gel onto a nitrocellulose membrane at 100 V for 60 min in cold transfer buffer.

steps.text: Wash the nitrocellulose membrane with 25 mL TBS for 5 min at room temperature.
steps.text_en: Wash the nitrocellulose membrane with 25 mL TBS for 5 min at room temperature.
steps.verbatim: 4. Wash the nitrocellulose membrane with 25 mL TBS for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.text: Drain off the TBS wash from the membrane.
steps.text_en: Drain off the TBS wash from the membrane.
steps.verbatim: 4. Wash the nitrocellulose membrane with 25 mL TBS for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.text: Add 25 mL of blocking buffer to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Add 25 mL of blocking buffer to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 5. Incubate the membrane in 25 mL of blocking buffer with gentle agitation for 1 hr at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate for 1 hr at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Incubate for 1 hr at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 5. Incubate the membrane in 25 mL of blocking buffer with gentle agitation for 1 hr at room temperature.

steps.text: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.
steps.text_en: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 6. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.
steps.text_en: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 6. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Add 10 mL primary antibody dilution buffer containing the primary antibody to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Add 10 mL primary antibody dilution buffer containing the primary antibody to the membrane with gentle agitation.

gaps.question: What dilution/concentration of primary antibody should be used?

steps.verbatim: 7. Incubate the membrane with primary antibody in 10 mL primary antibody dilution buffer with gentle agitation overnight at 4°C.

steps.text: Incubate overnight at 4°C with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Incubate overnight at 4°C with gentle agitation.

gaps.question: How many hours is 'overnight' at 4°C (assumed ~16 h)?

steps.verbatim: 7. Incubate the membrane with primary antibody in 10 mL primary antibody dilution buffer with gentle agitation overnight at 4°C.

steps.text: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.
steps.text_en: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 8. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.
steps.text_en: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 8. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Add HRP-conjugated secondary antibody at 1:2000 in 10 mL of blocking buffer to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Add HRP-conjugated secondary antibody at 1:2000 in 10 mL of blocking buffer to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 9. Incubate the membrane with the HRP-conjugated secondary antibody at 1:2000 in 10 mL of blocking buffer with gentle agitation for 1 hr at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate for 1 hr at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Incubate for 1 hr at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 9. Incubate the membrane with the HRP-conjugated secondary antibody at 1:2000 in 10 mL of blocking buffer with gentle agitation for 1 hr at room temperature.

steps.text: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.
steps.text_en: Add 15 mL of TBST to the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 10. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.
steps.text_en: Drain off the TBST wash from the membrane.

repeat.reason: wash three times for 5 min each

steps.verbatim: 10. Wash three times for 5 min each with 15 mL of TBST.

steps.text: Add 10 mL LumiGLO chemiluminescent reagent to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Add 10 mL LumiGLO chemiluminescent reagent to the membrane with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 11. Incubate the membrane with 10 mL LumiGLO chemiluminescent reagent with gentle agitation for 1 min at room temperature.

steps.text: Incubate for 1 min at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.text_en: Incubate for 1 min at room temperature with gentle agitation.
steps.verbatim: 11. Incubate the membrane with 10 mL LumiGLO chemiluminescent reagent with gentle agitation for 1 min at room temperature.

steps.text: Drain the membrane of excess developing solution (do not let it dry), wrap in plastic wrap, and expose to X-ray film. An initial 10 sec exposure should indicate the proper exposure time.
steps.text_en: Drain the membrane of excess developing solution (do not let it dry), wrap in plastic wrap, and expose to X-ray film. An initial 10 sec exposure should indicate the proper exposure time.
hazards: do not let the membrane dry
hazards_en: do not let the membrane dry

gaps.question: What is the proper X-ray film exposure time (to be determined empirically after the initial 10 sec exposure)?

steps.verbatim: 12. Drain the membrane of excess developing solution (do not let it dry), wrap in plastic wrap, and expose to X-ray film. An initial 10 sec exposure should indicate the proper exposure time.

open_parameters.question: What is the primary antibody and its working dilution/concentration?
How long is the 'overnight' primary antibody incubation at 4°C?
What is the proper X-ray film exposure time after the initial 10 sec exposure?
